ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Niŋi n na, o yeli o guruŋtumusi maa yipaara, “Ka m beeri Isawu tuusi ya soori, ka boosi i, ‘E yidaana n niŋi ŋani?’ koo ‘Ya n na e wara?’ koo ‘Ŋani buŋkobiri n ŋa?’,
translated: “If you see Isawu, ask him, ‘Why did you leave me?’, and ‘Where are you going?’, and ‘What is your problem?’”

ref: Joseph answered, “I'm looking for my brothers who are watching the sheep. Can you tell me where they are?”
text: Yisifu yeli u, “M beya n ta kpe, jaande, ka e baŋŋi seela ba ni ba buŋkobiri ni be, e wusi ma.”
translated: "This is the saying: 'If you see a person who is strong, brave, and skilled in fighting, respect them.'

ref: Egypt's seven years of plenty came to an end,
text: Yuma ayapoin seesi bindira n daa n niŋi ga maa jemani n daa n ti gaari,
translated: Yuma is going to the market to buy some food for his family.

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: O yeli u, “Pori n gaa o saani, a wa ti niŋi o guruŋu.”
translated: He said to him, "Go and tell the elder, I want to marry his daughter."

ref: The seven skinny, ugly cows that came up later also stand for seven years, as do the seven bad heads of grain that were scorched by the desert wind. The dreams mean there will be seven years when there won't be enough grain.
text: Nagikogiri ayapoin, dagseesi n lee n yi la niŋini yuma ayapoin, lala n niŋi chi nyogirinyogirisi ayapoin seesi sauwa n yi sagbinni ti pemsi a kuu la gba. A niŋini kom yuma ayapoin.
translated: The old man said, "If you want to be rich, don't be lazy. Work hard and you will be successful. You will be able to buy anything you want."
